elements.title: Nile
elements.contents: ... that the Nile (pictured) is the longest river in the world, but the volume of water it carries is only about 1% of the Amazon, 6% of the Congo, and 12% of the Yangtze?

elements.title: Antigoneia
elements.contents: ... that although one ancient source reports that the city of Antigoneia was destroyed in 300 BC, another records that it still existed almost 250 years later?

elements.title: Pope John Paul II's brother
elements.contents: ... that Pope John Paul II's brother died from scarlet fever after contracting it from one of his patients?

elements.title: repeal its school segregation laws
elements.contents: ... that Missouri did not repeal its school segregation laws until 22 years after Brown v. Board of Education?

elements.title: most-medaled SEA Games archer
elements.contents: ... that Singapore's "most-medaled SEA Games archer" was twice banned from competitive archery?

elements.title: 2013 Liechtenstein general election
elements.contents: ... that the 2013 Liechtenstein general election marked a shift from a two-party system towards a multi-party system?

elements.title: Husin Yusuf
elements.contents: ... that South Aceh regent Husin Yusuf removed his traditional ceremonial outfit in anger after the president of Indonesia failed to attend an exhibition of South Aceh's culture?

elements.title: Rudolstadt-Festival
elements.contents: ... that every year in July, a whole German town is taken over by the Rudolstadt-Festival and swept up in "folk fever"?

elements.title: Don McGowan
elements.contents: ... that Don McGowan once arranged a streaking incident on his talk show?
